title: Let It Go: Frozen Takes the World
story: Snowflakes swirled and hearts melted as Frozen hit theaters on November 27, 2013. This magical tale of two princess sisters and a lovable snowman became a global phenomenon. Featuring the voices of Idina Menzel and Kristen Bell, the movie's catchy songs and heartwarming story captivated audiences everywhere. Frozen went on to become the highest-grossing animated film of all time, proving that the power of sisterhood and catchy tunes can conquer all.
activity_question: Who were the two main voice actors mentioned in the story?
bonus_fact: The song Let It Go won an Oscar for Best Original Song.

title: The Prank That Paralyzed London
story: In 1809, a mischievous prankster named Theodore Hook hatched a wild plan. He bet a friend he could turn any address into London's hottest topic. On November 27, Hook unleashed chaos on quiet Berners Street. He sent hundreds of fake letters requesting deliveries and services. Soon, the street was jammed with confused workers, from chimney sweeps to cake makers. The prank worked too well, bringing central London to a standstill!

activity_choices: Turn any address into London's hottest topic
Bring cake to every house on Berners Street
Sweep all the chimneys in London
activity_answer: Turn any address into London's hottest topic
follow_up_question: Why do you think Hook's prank caused so much trouble for the city?

title: Mauna Loa Awakens: Hawaii's Fiery Giant
story: The ground rumbled and the sky glowed red as Mauna Loa roared to life in 2022. This massive volcano, Earth's largest active one, had been quietly sleeping for nearly 40 years. title: Hubble's Cosmic Discovery: Alien World's Air
story: Astronomers made an exciting discovery in 2001 on November 27. title: Norway's Underground Marvel
story: Imagine driving through a mountain for miles! In 2000, Norway unveiled an incredible feat of engineering on November 27. Engineers designed special lighting and rest areas to keep drivers alert and safe on the long journey beneath the earth.

Visitors would soon be able to step back into 18th-century America, seeing history come alive before their eyes.
activity_question: What was being restored in Virginia?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to restore historical places like Williamsburg?

title: A Red Flag Rises
story: Red as fire, with a fierce black eagle spread across it. That's the striking Albanian flag, first raised on November 27, 1912. After centuries under foreign rule, Albania declared independence and chose this bold symbol. The eagle represents strength and freedom, while the vivid red background stands for bravery and the blood of national heroes.
activity_question: What animal is featured on the Albanian flag?
follow_up_question: Why do you think Albania chose an eagle for their flag?

title: King Edward's Groundbreaking Parliament
story: King Edward I of England made a bold move in 1295. He called for a special meeting that would change how the country was run. This gathering became known as 'The Model Parliament'. It was special because it gave more power to the people's representatives. This meeting on November 27 helped shape the way governments work today.
activity_question: What was the name given to the special meeting called by King Edward I?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it was important to give more power to the people's representatives?

title: Uruguay's Big Step Forward
story: Cheers erupted across Uruguay as the country took a giant leap forward. On November 27, 1966, this South American nation adopted a brand-new constitution. This important document laid out the rules for how the country would be run, protecting people's rights and setting up a fair government. It was a proud moment for Uruguayans, marking a fresh start for their democracy.
activity_question: What did Uruguay adopt in 1966?
follow_up_question: Why do you think having a constitution is important for a country?
